sections.title: Masculine and Feminine Nouns
sections.explanation: Every French noun has a grammatical gender — either masculine or feminine. This affects the articles and adjectives used with the noun. Masculine nouns use the articles le (the) and un (a/an). Feminine nouns use la (the) and une (a/an). Before a vowel or silent h, both le and la shorten to l'. Common masculine endings include -eur, -isme, -age, -eau, -ment, and -et. Common feminine endings include -tion, -sion, -ure, -ette, -ance, -ence, -ée, and -ière. Many nouns have both a masculine and a feminine form: ami (male friend) becomes amie (female friend), and étudiant (male student) becomes étudiante (female student). Some words change meaning entirely depending on their gender: le livre means 'book' but la livre means 'pound (weight or currency)', and le tour means 'tour' or 'turn' while la tour means 'tower'.
sections.tip: When you learn a new French noun, always memorize it with its article (le or la). This is the most reliable way to learn gender, since spelling rules have many exceptions.

examples.en: a male student / a female student
the male dog / the female dog
the tour/turn vs the tower (meaning changes with gender)

exercises.prompt: Which article do you use with a masculine noun?
exercises.explanation: Masculine nouns use le (the) and un (a/an). Feminine nouns use la and une.

exercises.prompt: What is the correct article for 'maison' (house)?
exercises.explanation: 'Maison' is feminine. It takes the article la.

exercises.prompt: Which ending typically signals a feminine noun?
exercises.explanation: The ending -tion (as in nation, question) typically indicates a feminine noun.
'Le livre' (masculine) means 'the book'. 'La livre' (feminine) means 'the pound'.

exercises.prompt: Complete: ___ actrice est talentueuse. (The actress is talented.)
exercises.explanation: 'Actrice' is feminine and begins with a vowel, so le/la shortens to l'.

exercises.prompt: How do you make the masculine noun 'ami' (friend) feminine?
exercises.explanation: Many masculine nouns become feminine by adding -e: ami → amie.
'Problème' is masculine (le problème). 'Question' and 'situation' end in -tion/-sion and are feminine.

exercises.prompt: Complete: C'est ___ acteur célèbre. (He is a famous actor.)
exercises.explanation: 'Acteur' is masculine, so it takes the indefinite article 'un'.

sections.title: Making Nouns Plural
sections.explanation: To make most French nouns plural, you simply add -s to the end: ami → amis, livre → livres. This final -s is almost always silent in speech. Nouns that already end in -s, -x, or -z do not change in the plural: un bras (an arm) → des bras (arms), une voix (a voice) → des voix (voices). Nouns ending in -eau or -eu add -x instead of -s: un château (a castle) → des châteaux, un jeu (a game) → des jeux. Nouns ending in -al change their ending to -aux: un animal → des animaux, un journal → des journaux. For nouns ending in -ou, most add -s (un trou → des trous), but a small group of common nouns add -x instead (un bijou → des bijoux). A notable irregular plural is les yeux (eyes), whose singular form is l'œil.
sections.tip: Remember the -al → -aux pattern — it applies to many common words like animal, journal, and hôpital. Also memorise the short list of -ou nouns that take -x: bijou, caillou, chou, genou, hibou, joujou, pou.

examples.en: an arm → arms (no change: already ends in -s)
a jewel → jewels (special -ou noun that takes -x)

exercises.prompt: What is the plural of 'un livre' (a book)?
exercises.explanation: 'Livre' is a regular noun. It takes a simple -s in the plural: des livres.

exercises.prompt: What is the plural of 'un journal' (a newspaper)?
exercises.explanation: Nouns ending in -al change to -aux in the plural: journal → journaux.

exercises.prompt: What is the plural of 'un château' (a castle)?
exercises.explanation: Nouns ending in -eau add -x to form the plural: château → châteaux.

exercises.prompt: Complete: Les ___ sont beaux. (The eyes are beautiful.)
exercises.explanation: 'Œil' has an irregular plural: les yeux. It does not follow any standard rule.

exercises.prompt: What is the plural of 'une voix' (a voice)?
exercises.explanation: Nouns already ending in -x do not change in the plural: voix → voix.

exercises.prompt: What is the plural of 'un bijou' (a jewel)?
exercises.explanation: 'Bijou' is one of the special -ou nouns that add -x in the plural: bijoux.

exercises.prompt: Complete: Il a deux ___ dans le jardin. (He has two animals in the garden.)
exercises.explanation: The plural of 'animal' follows the -al → -aux pattern: animaux.

exercises.prompt: Which plural form is correct for 'un bras' (an arm)?
exercises.explanation: Nouns ending in -s do not change in the plural: bras → bras.
